text_excerpt:
EXAMINATION:  CHEST (PORTABLE AP)

INDICATION:  History: ___ with lung cancer, feeling weak, rule out infectious
cardiopulmonary process

TECHNIQUE:  Portable upright AP view of the chest

COMPARISON:  Chest CT ___, chest radiograph ___.

FINDINGS: 

Right-sided Port-A-Cath tip terminates in the mid SVC.  Heart size remains top
normal.  Mediastinal contour is unchanged with prominence of the pulmonary
arteries suggestive of underlying pulmonary arterial hypertension, better
assessed on the recent CT.  Left basilar opacification likely reflecting
combination of the patient's known necrotic left lower lobe mass and small
left pleural effusion is relatively similar compared to the previous
radiograph.  No new focal consolidation is present.  There is no pneumothorax.
Multiple clips are demonstrated within the medial aspect of the imaged left
upper extremity.

IMPRESSION: 

No significant interval change from the previous radiograph.  Continued left
basilar opacification reflective of the patient's known necrotic left lower
lobe mass and small left pleural effusion.
